finding: ATM Team Broker can mark another captain's task as file-scope parallel-safe, but a second captain can still be unable to progress an unrelated governance task when unmodeled global resources are busy. The missing abstraction is not five new locks; runner-sync/build, release mirror, Git index/staging, branch commit queue, and generated projection ownership are not yet projected through the same unified admission decision as source overlap.
expectedBehavior: ATM-GOV-0137 unified admission should become the single projection that answers source overlap, index ownership, release surface, queue position, generated projection ownership, owner TTL/heartbeat, and suggested next action. Existing close-window, branch commit queue, index lane, close-transaction-mutex, and Broker shared-surface mechanisms should migrate or alias into this Broker keyspace instead of creating another lock semantics. Runner-sync/build should use a single coalescing steward lane with sealed source-SHA requests, while journal-style backlog writes should only append item shards and leave generated projection rebuilds to a steward or commit-time owner lane.
evidenceOrRepro: On 2026-07-15, TASK-RFT-0039 held an active broker intent for team-legacy.ts, team/legacy/**/*.ts, tests/cli/team-*.test.ts, and the atom-map owner shard. A second captain needed to import/claim ATM-GOV-0147 to fix residue cleanup and missing error-code registry entries, but node atm.mjs reported ATM_RUNNER_SYNC_REQUIRED. The required ATM_RETAIN_RELEASE_ARTIFACTS=1 npm run build would touch release/atm-onefile, release/atm-root-drop, and Team/RFT release mirrors, so the safe path became waiting for TASK-RFT-0039 even though the intended source fix was outside the RFT scope. A prompt asking to record this backlog routed to TASK-RFT-0039 because active RFT task scope dominated natural-language routing; a later prompt to update this item routed to already-closed ATM-GOV-0137 because follow-up task IDs dominated the backlog-update intent.
followUp: Attach this follow-up to the residual gaps of ATM-GOV-0137 unified admission, ATM-GOV-0142 release steward, and ATM-GOV-0128 index convergence rather than creating a parallel family. Required design: project existing global coordination mechanisms into one Broker admission projection; model runner-sync/build as a coalescing steward queue with waiting task ids, sealed source SHA, TTL/heartbeat, stale-owner release, and owner/queue-position diagnostics; move generated Markdown projection rebuilds out of the hot journaling path so backlog item JSON writes are append-only and low-conflict; add a true dual-Captain E2E fixture where actor A holds an RFT Team surface and actor B safely records a backlog item or claims a non-overlapping governance task without manual index operations; ensure blocked admission reports owner, resource key, queue position, expiry, and recommended command instead of only sleeping and failing.
